channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Yemen's Houthis signal they'll now limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to Israeli ships
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels have signaled they now will limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to only Israeli-affiliated ships, just as a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip entered its second day Monday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: White House adviser says Trump team provided 'significant support' in ceasefire deal
item.description: White House principal deputy national security adviser Jon Finer said on Sunday that President-elect Trump’s team provided “significant support” in the recent ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas. “We got significant support from the Trump team.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Nigerian gasoline tanker explosion death toll rises to 86, with 55 others injured
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from a gasoline tanker explosion in north-central Nigeria has risen to 86, the country’s emergency response agency said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bittersweet rejoicing as first hostages return to Israel after 471 days in captivity
item.description: Three hostages return to Israel after 471 days in Hamas captivity under a cease-fire deal. Israelis celebrate their return but face concerns about the release of convicted Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German ambassador warns Trump will 'undermine' democratic principles with 'maximum disruption' agenda: report
item.description: In a confidential briefing document, Germany's ambassador to the United States Andreas Michaelis warned that Donald Trump will "undermine" democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes effect ahead of hostage release
item.description: Fighting in Gaza halted on Sunday as the ceasefire deal negotiated went into effect, ending the 15-month-long war between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Palestinians trek across rubble to return to their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes hold
item.description: Even before the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas was fully in place, Palestinians in the war-battered Gaza Strip began to return to the remains of the homes they had evacuated during the 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: Samy Magdy And Wafaa Shurafa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Warm weather threatens Epiphany ice water plunges for Russia's Orthodox Christians
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Thousands of Russians celebrating the Orthodox Christian feast day of Epiphany, where worshippers bathe in the icy waters of frozen lakes and rivers, have been forced to cancel their traditional ceremonies amid unusually warm winter temperatures.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southeast Asian foreign ministers seek breakthrough in Myanmar conflict and South China Sea disputes
item.description: LANGKAWI, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers gathered Sunday for their first meeting this year under the regional bloc's new chair, Malaysia, seeking a breakthrough over Myanmar’s drawn-out civil war and territorial disputes in the South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu praises Trump in 'temporary' ceasefire announcement
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu praised President-elect Trump for his role in negotiating a ceasefire between the nation and the militant group Hamas. “From the moment he was elected, President Trump has been involved in the mission for the release of our hostages.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — After more than a year of devastating war, the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas is set to begin on Sunday, pausing the 15-month conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exploring Greenland's majestic history as President-elect Trump shines spotlight on island nation
item.description: Greenland is the world's largest island, but most of it is covered by ice. Its southern tip, however, is sprinkled with picturesque towns and villages that are isolated from each other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump inherits fragile Gaza ceasefire
item.description: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, expected to begin its first phase Sunday, has brought relief to the international community but questions about whether the truce will hold — an issue that President-elect Trump will have to oversee when he takes office next week.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Stefanik wages Democratic charm offensive ahead of confirmation hearing
item.description: Senate Democrats appear to be warming up to Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.), President-elect Trump’s combative and confrontational nominee for envoy to the United Nations, encouraged by commitments she’ll engage with the global body rather than seek to burn it down.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump vowed to end the war in Ukraine quickly but Moscow and Kyiv are digging in before any talks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has pledged to broker a peace deal in Ukraine, but as he prepares to take office, peace seems as elusive as ever. Moscow and Kyiv are seeking battlefield gains to strengthen their negotiating positions ahead of any prospective talks to end the 3-year-old war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 40 Pakistanis feared drowned in the capsizing of a migrant boat off African coast
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — More than 40 Pakistanis were feared to have drowned in the capsizing of a boat off West Africa's Atlantic coastline, which has emerged as a primary point of departure for migrants aiming to reach Europe.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming British ambassador to US praises Trump in op-ed
item.description: The incoming British ambassador to the U.S. Peter Mandelson praised President-elect Trump in a new op-ed, predicting his forthcoming presidency will be one of the “most consequential” in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: UN announces new plan to counter the surge in antisemitism
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United Nations announced new actions Friday to counter the surge in antisemitism, including encouraging governments to enforce laws against hate crimes and discrimination. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel's full Cabinet approves the Gaza ceasefire and hostage release deal
item.description: Israel's Cabinet has approved the Gaza ceasefire deal that would pause the fighting and release dozens of hostages held by militants, along with Palestinians imprisoned by Israel. The approval came early on Saturday after a marathon session by the full Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli government approves ceasefire deal: Reports
item.description: The Israeli government has formally approved the ceasefire deal in Gaza and the release of some hostages, soon halting the conflict that has been raging for 15 months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daughter of one of the oldest Israeli hostages hopes for answers in ceasefire deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Sharone Lifschitz is well aware that the odds are against her 84-year-old father. As one of the oldest hostages taken by Hamas, Oded Lifshitz would be among the first to be released under a ceasefire deal expected to begin Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lithuania vows to boost defense spending to 5-6% of GDP, citing the threat of Russian aggression
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — Lithuania has decided to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of overall national economic output starting in 2026 due to the threat of Russian aggression in the region, Lithuanian President Gitanas Nausėda said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LIUDAS DAPKUS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Anti-AIDS program in GOP crosshairs over abortion funding
item.description: Republicans are fuming at the State Department after learning that recipients of funding from the legacy program to curb HIV/AIDS across Africa, PEPFAR, performed abortions in violation of U.S. law. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia, Iran to seal partnership treaty days before Trump takes office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian will meet in Moscow to sign a partnership pact as the two nations brace for President-elect Trump's return.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American college rape suspect is being extradited to the US
item.description: Ian Cleary, who is accused of sexually assaulting a Gettysburg College student at a party in 2013, is being sent back to the U.S., officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden to lift Cuba terrorism designation, reversing Trump decision in final week in office
item.description: The Biden administration announced on Tuesday that it will remove Cuba from the list of State Sponsors of Terrorism (SST) in exchange for the release of political prisoners jailed for protesting against the regime in July 2021.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: VIDEO: 84-year-old man fights off robber using pair of jeans
item.description: YORKSHIRE, U.K. (WFLA) — An 84-year-old man used a pair of jeans to fight off a would-be robber as he did his laundry in South Yorkshire.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexico sends firefighters to Los Angeles to help battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Disaster relief workers from the National Forestry Commission and the Ministry of Defense in Mexico also arrived to assist local emergency workers.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
